data.title: Oroco Resource amends up to C$1.5M private placement of units
data.description:
• Oroco Resource (TSXV:OCO:CA) (OTCQB:ORRCF) announced on Wednesday certain amendments to the terms of its non-brokered private placement.
  • The company is now offering up to 6M units at C$0.25 per unit to raise gross proceeds of up to C$1.5M. The previous announcement said up to 5M units priced at C$0.30 per unit.
  • Each unit will consist of one share and one share purchase warrant. Each warrant entitles the holder to purchase one share at C$0.40.
  • The proceeds are intended to be used for the advancement of the Santo Tomás Project located in Chihuahua State, Mexico, as well as working capital and other general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Artisan Partners signals growth in emerging markets debt and private real estate strategies for 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Artisan Partners Asset Management Inc. (NYSE:APAM) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Eric Colson highlighted the company's 30-year history, emphasizing its focus on building a high-value investment platform that supports talent and drives long-term growth. He noted that Artisan now manages over $160 billion across 11 investment teams and 25 strategies. Thirteen of these strategies achieved net inflows in 2024, with 10 exceeding $100 million in net inflows.
  • President Jason Gottlieb provided updates on the firm's two credit-oriented franchises. The Denver-based credit team raised $1.7 billion in net inflows in 2024, while the Boston-based EMsights Capital Group raised $1.9 billion. Together, the two teams now manage nearly $15 billion across six strategies, including alternatives. Performance for these strategies included annualized returns of 10.41% and 9.76% for absolute return-oriented strategies.
  • CFO C.J. Daley stated, "Revenues rose 6%, adjusted operating income was up 12%, and our adjusted operating margin improved by 180 basis points compared to the September quarter." He also noted that $17 million in performance fees were recognized during the quarter.

Outlook

  • Management highlighted key milestones for 2025, including the 10th anniversary of the Developing World strategy and the three-year anniversaries of several EMsights strategies. These milestones are expected to increase the addressable market for pooled vehicles and other investment products.
  • The company is advancing in private markets, with later-stage opportunities in private real estate receiving increased focus. Gottlieb emphasized Artisan’s methodical approach to expanding its platform and partnering with the right talent, adding that the firm remains open to exploring new opportunities across asset classes.
  • Daley provided expense guidance, projecting fixed expenses to increase mid- to low single digits in 2025. Seasonal expenses in Q1 are expected to rise by $6 million compared to Q4 2024.

Financial Results

  • Revenues for Q4 2024 increased 6% sequentially and 19% compared to the same quarter last year. Adjusted net income per adjusted share improved 14% sequentially and 35% year-over-year.
  • Assets under management (AUM) ended the quarter at $161 billion, down 4% sequentially but up 7% year-over-year. Net client cash outflows during the quarter totaled $800 million, including a $1.1 billion outflow from a client rebalance.
  • Performance fees for the quarter reached $17 million, with $12 million earned in the fourth quarter alone. The weighted average fee rate, including performance fees, was 72 basis points.
  • For the full year 2024, revenues increased 14% compared to 2023, while adjusted operating income rose 22%. Dividends declared with respect to 2024 totaled $3.48 per share, representing an 8% dividend yield.

Q&A

  • Bill Katz, TD Cowen, asked about fee rate dynamics and capacity management. CEO Colson explained that foundational assets in emerging markets debt and global unconstrained strategies had temporarily lowered fee rates but are critical for long-term growth. He also noted that strategies like international value are carefully managed for capacity to protect performance.
  • Alex Blostein, Goldman Sachs, questioned how volatility in global and emerging markets affects client behavior. Colson observed hesitation among clients due to macroeconomic uncertainties but highlighted strategic asset allocation adjustments as a potential growth area. Gottlieb added that Artisan’s strategies are well-positioned to capitalize on volatility-driven opportunities.
  • John Dunn, Evercore ISI, asked about upcoming strategy milestones. Gottlieb identified the three-year anniversaries of EMsights strategies and the International Explorer strategy in 2025 as significant growth opportunities.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on capacity management and fee rate dynamics. They raised concerns about macroeconomic uncertainty but acknowledged the firm’s progress in credit and alternatives.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing long-term growth, operational leverage, and strong investment performance. Colson stated, "We are confident in the foundational growth of our emerging markets debt strategies."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts appeared slightly more positive due to improved financial performance and increased performance fees. Management’s tone remained consistent, underscoring its strategic focus on scaling new platforms and maintaining operational efficiency.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management’s tone and strategic focus were consistent with the prior quarter, but the current quarter emphasized milestones for EMsights and private real estate.
  • Analysts maintained a focus on expense management and capacity, though they expressed greater optimism about performance fees and growth in newer strategies compared to the previous quarter.
  • Financial results improved sequentially, with higher revenues, adjusted operating income, and performance fees driving stronger overall performance in Q4 2024.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified macroeconomic uncertainties and global market volatility as key challenges. Colson noted that client hesitation stems from inflation and regulatory concerns.
  • Analysts raised concerns about fee rate compression and capacity constraints in mature strategies.
  • Management outlined mitigation strategies, including careful capacity management, operational leverage, and diversification into private markets.

Final Takeaway

Artisan Partners delivered strong financial performance in Q4 2024, supported by growth in performance fees and disciplined expense management. The firm’s focus on scaling its emerging markets debt and private real estate strategies positions it for long-term growth. Management remains confident in its ability to navigate macroeconomic challenges while leveraging its investment platform to expand capacity and deliver value to shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Qualcomm in charts: Automotive grows most Y/Y among QCT revenue streams in FQ1
Crown Holdings' Q4 results top street view, sets bullish outlook
data.description:
Driven by increased volumes in its beverage can segment, Crown Holdings (NYSE:CCK) reported better-than-expected results in the fourth quarter and set its profit outlook for FY25 above Wall Street’s consensus estimates.

Shares raced higher after Wednesday’s close, last trading hands with a gain of more than 7% and setting up the stock to trade in the green during Thursday’s regular session after a string of losses.

"Crown delivered strong results in the fourth quarter, with segment income advancing 12% compared to the prior year," stated Crown CEO Timothy Donahue.

In the final quarter of the year, Crown earned an above consensus adjusted profit of $1.59 per share, up 28% from last year, on a 1.4% gain in revenue to $2.9B. Total revenue was $10M more than expected.

The company generated record adjusted free cash flow of $814M, supported by earnings growth combined with normalized capital expenditures. During the year, Crown (NYSE:CCK) reduced net debt by $878M and ended 2024 with net leverage of 2.7x.

Adjusted earnings for FY25 are expected to increase by 6.1% at the midpoint of the $6.60 to $7.00 range from FY24, above the $6.83 estimate.

The company guides Q1 adjusted EPS at $1.20 to $1.30 per share, versus $1.15 estimates.

data.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacifico reports preliminary January passenger traffic increase
data.description:
• Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico, S.A.B. de C.V. (NYSE: PAC) announces preliminary terminal passenger traffic figures for January 2025, compared with January 2024.
  • For January 2025, the total number of terminal passengers at GAP’s 12 Mexican airports increased by 6.3%, compared to January 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Crédit Agricole targets 12% ROTE for 2025 as it exceeds 2024 profitability goals
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Crédit Agricole S.A. (OTCPK:CRARF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Deputy CEO Jerome Grivet highlighted that Crédit Agricole exceeded all 2025 targets a year early, with net profit 20% above the target, return on tangible equity (ROTE) 2 percentage points higher, and the cost/income ratio significantly below the 58% ceiling. This marks the third consecutive year the group has surpassed its medium-term plan goals ahead of schedule.
  • A record level of profitability was achieved, supported by robust revenue growth in all business lines. Dividend per share is proposed to increase by 5% to EUR1.1.
  • Asset Management and Insurance activities reported record levels, with Amundi achieving EUR55 billion in net inflows and EUR2.24 trillion in assets under management.
  • Grivet noted "stabilization of the deposit mix in France," which is expected to improve the net interest margin further.
  • The cost of risk increased due to IFRS 9 provisions, though Stage 3 provisions decreased, reflecting stable asset quality.

Outlook

  • Grivet indicated that a new medium-term plan with updated targets extending to 2028 would likely be shared in Q4 2025. For 2025, the group continues to target a ROTE above 12%, a cost/income ratio below 58%, and a strong net profit comparable to 2024 levels.
  • The focus on securitization and leveraging the Capital Markets Union (CMU) was highlighted as a potential growth avenue in the next strategic plan.
  • Management emphasized that geopolitical and macroeconomic uncertainties would be monitored but are not expected to disrupt current profitability trends.

Financial Results

  • Crédit Agricole posted record Q4 and full-year profitability levels. ROTE reached 14% in 2024, the highest in its history.
  • Revenue for Q4 2024 rose sharply by 17.4% on a stated basis and 18.2% on an underlying basis, driven by robust performance in asset gathering and insurance activities, strong CIB division results, and an improved margin in consumer finance.
  • The cost/income ratio improved due to slowing cost growth, which increased by only 5.6% on a stated basis and 4.4% on an underlying basis.
  • The solvency ratio remained strong at 11.7%, with CET1 ratio stability supported by retained earnings and prudent management of risk-weighted assets.

Q&A

  • Tarik El Mejjad, Bank of America: Asked about structural shifts in ROTE and whether the bank may embed CMU strategies in its plans. Jerome Grivet: Reaffirmed the 2025 ROTE target of over 12% and stated that CMU-related securitization opportunities would be explored in the new medium-term plan.
  • Giulia Aurora Miotto, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about excess capital deployment strategies and asset quality in France. Grivet: Emphasized bolt-on acquisitions over transformational deals and confirmed stable asset quality despite macroeconomic pressures.
  • Sharath Kumar, Deutsche Bank: Questioned the improvement in French mortgage loan production and consumer finance trends. Grivet: Indicated that mortgage loan production rebounded 18% year-on-year in Q4, while consumer finance margins showed positive momentum.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed positive sentiment regarding the record ROTE of 14% and the bank's ability to exceed targets consistently. However, some skepticism was noted about the broader macroeconomic impacts and regulatory risks.
  • Management displayed confidence in prepared remarks and Q&A, underscored by strong performance metrics and strategic clarity. Phrases such as "we are well-positioned" and "we are confident" reflected optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remained consistently positive, with analysts focusing on future growth rather than immediate operational challenges.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained consistent, with no significant changes in targets for 2025. The focus on exceeding medium-term goals ahead of schedule continued.
  • Strategic priorities shifted slightly toward exploring CMU-related securitization opportunities.
  • Analysts focused on France's asset quality and the implications of higher capital buffers, a shift from the previous quarter's emphasis on revenue growth.
  • Management confidence remained high, supported by strong financial results and a clear path toward achieving 2025 targets.

Risks and Concerns

  • The cost of risk increased due to IFRS 9 provisions, though management stressed that Stage 3 provisions decreased, indicating stable asset quality.
  • Regulatory uncertainties, such as Basel 4 impacts and changes in CET1 requirements, were flagged as potential challenges.
  • Geopolitical risks, particularly in Italy, were acknowledged, though management expressed confidence in defending the bank’s interests.

Final Takeaway

Crédit Agricole delivered record profitability in 2024, exceeding its medium-term targets well ahead of schedule. Strong revenue growth across all business lines, particularly in asset management and insurance, supported these results. The bank reiterated its 2025 targets, including a ROTE above 12%, while preparing a new medium-term plan for 2028. With a robust solvency position and a focus on strategic initiatives like securitization, Crédit Agricole remains well-positioned for sustained growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: TTM Technologies Non-GAAP EPS of $0.60 beats by $0.13, revenue of $651M beats by $20.89M
data.description:
• TTM Technologies press release (NASDAQ:TTMI): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.60 beats by $0.13.
  • Revenue of $651M (+14.4% Y/Y) beats by $20.89M.
  • Shares +10.98% AH.
  • Business OutlookFor the first quarter of 2025, TTM estimates that revenues will be in the range of $600 million to $640 million, vs. $603.55M consensus, and non-GAAP net income will be in the range of $0.37 to $0.43 per diluted share, vs. $0.37 consensus.

data.title: WEX Non-GAAP EPS of $3.57 beats by $0.02, revenue of $637M beats by $2.41M
data.description:
• WEX press release (NYSE:WEX): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.57 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $637M (-3.9% Y/Y) beats by $2.41M.
  • Shares -2.82% AH.
  • Financial Guidance
  • For the first quarter of 2025, the Company expects revenue in the range of $625 million to $640 million, vs. $661.18M consensus, and adjusted net income in the range of $132 million to $138 million, or $3.35 to $3.50 per diluted share, vs. $3.78 consensus.
  • For the full year 2025, the Company expects revenue in the range of $2.60 billion to $2.66 billion, vs. $2.71B consensus, and adjusted net income in the range of $571 million to $595 million, or $14.65 to $15.25 per diluted share, vs. $16.79 consensus.

data.title: Insurer Markel Group to start business review
data.description:
Markel Group (NYSE:MKL) announced it will conduct a review of its business.

The board will lead the review, helped by external consultants and advisors, the company said in a statement on Wednesday. The insurer will also review its structure, capital allocation and enhance its disclosures.

The review comes after activist investor Jana Partners in December said Markel (NYSE:MKL) should explore a separation or sale of its private investments business. Jana also said it believes the entire company could be a good takeover target for larger insurers.

"In December 2024, JANA Partners publicly shared their perspectives on Markel Group and offered suggestions we might consider," Markel said in the statement. "We took this as an opportunity for broader self-reflection, in line with our commitment to the "zealous pursuit of excellence."

data.title: Strange twists as the Luka Doncic trade, Las Vegas Sands and the Texas Governor intersect
data.description:
The NBA world was sent spinning last weekend when Luka Doncic was traded to the Los Angeles Lakers in a three-team deal that saw the Mavericks bring in Anthony Davis and two other players. The shock factor from the trade centered on the 25-year old Doncic being widely considered a generational talent at just 25 years old after having already earned five All-NBA First Team selections and leading the Mavericks to the NBA Finals last season.

The blockbuster trade prompted some fans to speculate that Mavericks' owner Miriam Adelson approved the trade to gain leverage in Texas to get a gambling bill approved by the legislature. The theory was that a threat to move the team to Las Vegas would seem more legitimate if the Mavericks fan base was dissatisfied. Of note, the NBA has long been expected to enter Las Vegas when it inevitably expands from 30 to 32 teams.

Adelson, who is the majority shareholder of Las Vegas Sands (NYSE:LVS), purchased a majority ownership stake of the Mavericks from Mark Cuban along with Sands President and Chief Operating Officer Patrick Dumont. Another twist to the story is that Las Vegas Sands (NYSE:LVS) is missing a presence in Las Vegas after selling the Venetian Resort Las Vegas and the Sands Expo and Convention Center to affiliates of Apollo Global Management (APO) and VICI Properties (VICI) in 2022. It is unclear what Adelson and Las Vegas Sands (NYSE:LVS) will do if efforts to bring legalized gambling to the Lone Star state fall short.

The Vegas theory was refuted by the Dallas Mavericks ownership group on Wednesday. "The Adelson and Dumont families have already started and are committed to investing and building in Dallas/Fort Worth," noted the owners. "The families have absolutely no plans to move the team out of North Texas."

However, a new piece of the puzzle emerged when Texas Governor Greg Abbott said on a podcast that he now supports online sports betting in Texas. In remarkable timing, it is the first time Abbott has said he supports any type of legalized gambling in Texas. Legalized gambling and sports betting in Texas is of high interest to Las Vegas Sands (LVS), MGM Resorts International (MGM), FanDuel (FLU), Landry's Golden Nugget and other casino and hospitality companies, with the population in the state now over 30 million.
